questions.question: Explain the three types of loops in C with their syntax.
keyPoints: Difference: while checks first, do-while executes at least once

questions.question: What is the difference between break and continue statements in C loops?
keyPoints: continue: skips current iteration and moves to next
continue jumps to loop condition check

questions.question: Write a C function to generate delay in 8051 using nested for loops.
keyPoints: Typical: void delay(unsigned int cnt) { unsigned int i,j; for(i=0;i<cnt;i++) for(j=0;j<127;j++); }

questions.question: List and explain four applications of loops in 8051 microcontroller programming.
What is an infinite loop? How is it created in C and where is it used in 8051?
keyPoints: Creation: for(;;) or while(1) or while(true)
In 8051: used as main program loop
May use break or reset to exit

questions.question: Explain nested loops with an example relevant to 8051.
keyPoints: Nested loop: one loop inside another
Example: nested for loops for time delay
Used in display multiplexing, delay generation

questions.question: Compare while and do-while loops with suitable examples for 8051 applications.
keyPoints: while: condition checked first, may not execute
do-while: condition checked after, always executes once
Example while: polling sensor in while(1)
Example do-while: display menu at least once

questions.question: Write a complete C program for 8051 to interface a push button and LED using loop structures. The LED should blink continuously while the button is pressed.
keyPoints: Define SFR addresses for P1 (LED), P3 (button)
Create delay function using nested loops
Main loop: while(1) check button status
If button pressed: toggle LED with delay
If button not pressed: LED off
Use bit-level access for button and LED

questions.question: Design a 4-digit 7-segment display multiplexing program for 8051 using nested loop structures. Explain the logic.
keyPoints: Use nested loops: outer selects digit, inner sends pattern

questions.question: Explain the working of keypad scanning using loops in 8051. Write pseudo-code for a 4x4 matrix keypad interface.
keyPoints: Matrix keypad: rows and columns arrangement
Scanning: set row LOW, read columns
Loop structure: for each row, set LOW
Check if any column becomes LOW
Identify pressed key from row-column combination

questions.question: Discuss loop control statements (break, continue, goto) in C with examples. Compare their usage in embedded systems versus general programming.
keyPoints: In embedded: avoid goto for readability
Use break for finite exit conditions
Use continue for skipping invalid data
Timer-based exit preferred over infinite loops
